request_text: Raise the number of concurrent sessions per user from 3 to 10
note: Clause 3.1 caps concurrent sessions at 3; the TARGET of the from-to pair (10) exceeds it. Today the extractor takes the first digit (3) and 3 > 3 never fires (the M3-09 miss).

request_text: Reduce concurrent sessions per user from 6 to 2
note: Decrease control: the target (2) is within the clause 3.1 limit (3) — proves the pair fix must take the TARGET, not the max of the two numbers. Session management itself is in scope.

request_text: Generate 10 standard reports per month instead of 5
note: English 'X instead of Y' puts the target FIRST, so today's first-digit rule already reads 10 — regression guard: the pair fix must not break it. 10 exceeds the clause 4.2.1 limit (5/month).
Turkish from-to pair (ablative 5'ten → dative 8'e): the target (8) exceeds the clause 4.2.1 limit (5/month). Today the first digit (5) is taken and 5 > 5 never fires.

request_text: Kullanıcı başına eşzamanlı oturum sayısı 3’ten 10’a yükseltilsin
note: Same as QT-04 on the sessions clause, with CURLY apostrophes (U+2019) as real Turkish text uses — the pair regex must accept both apostrophe forms. Target 10 > limit 3.
Apostrophe-less ASCII-typed Turkish (5ten 8e, cikarilsin): the pair must still resolve to the target (8 > 5, clause 4.2.1).
Turkish 'X yerine Y' puts the target SECOND — today the first digit (5) is taken and the quota never fires. Target 12 > clause 4.2.1 limit (5/month).
Dative with buffer consonant (6'ya): the Turkish pair regex must cover -a/-e/-ya/-ye. Target 6 > clause 3.1 limit (3).

request_text: Add 2 of the six standard report templates
note: Digit-beats-word priority control (pinned in unit tests): quantity is 2, not six — within the clause 4.2.1 limit, and report work is in scope.
Equal-to-limit control: 3 > 3 is false, the quota branch must NOT fire — the contract already grants exactly 3 (clause 3.1).

request_text: Update the reporting library to version 4
note: Dependency-change control: 'version 4' must never be read as a quantity (no CR-by-limit). The corpus declares no manifests, so an upgrade of an undeclared package routes to GRAY per the 1b branch.
Turkish decrease control: target 4 is within the clause 4.2.1 limit (5/month) — the pair fix must take 4, not 8, and not flag a breach.
